summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of an above‑average Spring ’90 show highlighted by a brooding Death Don't Have No Mercy and a king‑size Drum segment.
review: The recording, sourced from the Miller upgrade soundboard, is clear and well‑balanced with no reported flaws. Musically the concert sits solidly in the middle of the 1990 Atlanta run – not the peak of the era but consistently strong, especially the second set’s Drums, The Other One and a powerful Death Don't Have No Mercy. Standout moments like Brown Eyed Women and Brent’s jazzy keyboards add extra sparkle.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers gave 4‑star ratings and praised the performance while noting only minor set‑list quirks; recording is described as a Miller upgrade soundboard with no issues, yielding an overall strong score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller upgrade (12198) source; reviewers mention crisp sound and no audible drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A solid first set with a few rough edges, followed by a stronger second set that builds calm satisfaction and peaks with an excellent Drum/Space segment leading into The Other One and a brooding Death Don't.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s playing described as “magic” on Brown Eyed Women and generally strong throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir praised as "Bobby is great" during the first set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland noted for jazzy, swinging keyboards that add flavor to several tunes.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers highlighted; Drums segment called “king” and a peak of ’90.
